instructions.purpose: Verify each listing is a real, currently-active mahjong event by visiting the URLs and confirming the event details match what we have on file.
steps: For each listing, visit every URL in urlsToCheck.
Confirm the event exists, is still active/scheduled, and the details (venue, schedule, contact) match our listing.
Set audit.status to one of: verified, cannot_verify, dead_link, event_ended, info_mismatch, suspicious.
If info doesn't match, put the correct values in audit.correctedFields (e.g. { "address": "new address" }).
Set audit.checkedAt to the current date.
For listings with hasNoUrls=true, try searching Google for the organizer name + city.

auditStatuses.cannot_verify: No working URL found, couldn't confirm via search either
auditStatuses.event_ended: Event existed but is no longer running
auditStatuses.info_mismatch: Event exists but our details are wrong — see correctedFields
auditStatuses.suspicious: Listing looks fake, duplicated, or is not actually a mahjong event

correctedFields.frequencyCorrection: This is almost certainly annual or irregular, not weekly. Frequency should not be weekly for a charity tournament.

listings.name: Masters & Mahj Open Play
listings.description:
Join Mahjong Mama NWA for a morning of Open Play at Neighborhood Workspace — an exclusive morning dedicated to connection, community, and plenty of mahj.

This event is open to all experience levels. Please note this is not a lesson. Mahjong Mama will be available to answer questions as needed, but players should have a basic understanding of gameplay. No need to be an experienced player — this is the perfect setting to build confidence and get more comfortable at the table. NMJL rules, blanks, messy, or towers are all welcomed. Laugh, Learn, Play... Your Way!

What to expect:

Mahjong setups provided
Light snacks and beverages included
Curated raffle prizes given throughout the morning
Bring your 2026 NMJL card or borrow one there
Big fun, good company, and a whole lot of mahj — see you at the table!

audit.notes: Masters & Mahj Open Play, Tontitown AR, April 10 — future. Active NWA mahjong event in Tontitown (Washington County).

listings.name: Mahjong Class for Beginners — Central Valley & Online
listings.address: Central Valley (location varies; contact for Fresno-area sessions)
listings.description: Friendly beginner-focused American Mah Jongg classes taught by Connie of the Mahjong Academy. Classes are available in-person in the SF Bay Area and Central Valley, or online. listings.name: Secret Mahjong Society — SF Events

Recent confirmed dates: "The Secret Lantern" March 14 2026, "The First Draw" Dec 6 2025, "Phantom Draw" Oct 18 2025. Rotating tables, DJ sets, instructors. Also operates in SJ and LA.

listings.name: SF Japanese Mahjong Meetup

listings.name: SFPL — Mahjong for Beginners (Sunset Branch)
listings.description: Free beginner mahjong classes at SFPL's Sunset Branch. Registration required. Two-hour sessions for new and returning players.

audit.notes: Confirmed via sfpl.org. Monthly beginner mahjong class at Sunset Branch (1305 18th Ave), 10:15am–12:15pm. Feb 28 2026 event confirmed on sfpl.org. Registration required; call (415) 355-2808. December 2025 session was fully booked, indicating strong demand.

listings.name: The Jay Hotel — Mahjong & Martinis

audit.notes: Confirmed via Eventbrite and downtownsf.org. The Jay Hotel Mahjong & Martinis (with Mahjong Movement) runs every other Thursday 4:30–6:30pm on the hotel's 3rd-floor terrace. FREE event. April 9 2026 event confirmed on Eventbrite. 4 open-play tables, martini menu, instructors available.

listings.name: UN Plaza — Free Sunday Mahjong

audit.notes: UN Plaza free Sunday mahjong (11am–2pm) was confirmed active by SF Parks Alliance and sfciviccenter.org, but one source noted the program was "extended through March 9 2025" suggesting it may have been a time-limited initiative. Cannot confirm the program continued into 2026. sfrecpark.org has a "Chess and Mahjong" calendar entry that may be related. Recommend checking sfciviccenter.org for 2026 dates.

listings.name: YLL Mahjong Club — Bay Area Parties

YLL (Youth Luck Leisure) Mahjong Club hosts bimonthly parties with up to 30 tables/200 guests in SF restaurants, bars, nightclubs. Founded by Ryan Lee (age ~25). Events sell out quickly. Expanding to LA. Very active in 2025–2026.

listings.name: Mahjong + Yoga Retreat
listings.description:
Hi everyone 😄 We are so excited to Introduce the HobbyCon Mahjong + Yoga Retreat this Memorial Day Weekend!

📆 May 22-25, 2026
📍 Long Island, NY

Step away from the noise and spend a weekend on the Long Island Sound learning, relaxing, and connecting with 8-10 fellow hobby lovers. We have partnered with The Charleston Club Mahjong to bring you the best playing experience possible.

Your weekend will include:
🀄 Step-by-step American Mahjong lessons
🧘‍♀️ Relaxing yoga sessions for all levels
🍴 Breakfast, lunch, and dinner plus drinks daily
🎲 Plenty of Mahjong play time to practice and improve
🍇 A vineyard excursion with great people who share your curiosity
🏠 Luxury accommodations including hot tub, fire pit, & waterfront views

No experience needed! Just come ready to learn, unwind, and enjoy the experience.

🔗 Learn more and reserve your spot:
http://hobbycon.com/retreats

listings.name: Mahj for Meals – Annual Fundraiser (Tarrant Area Food Bank)

audit.notes: Confirmed — Mahj for Meals is a well-established annual mahjong fundraiser for Tarrant Area Food Bank. 10th annual event in 2025 raised over $300,000 at River Crest Country Club. Founded 2016 by Mary Ralph Lowe and Elizabeth Ray. Active event page at tafb.org/event/mahj-for-meals. Covered in PaperCity Magazine. Highly legitimate charity event.
